skill.name: architecture-patterns
skill.description: Implement proven backend architecture patterns including Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, and Domain-Driven Design. Use when architecting complex backend systems or refactoring existing codebases for better maintainability and testability.
skill.summary: Implement proven backend architecture patterns including Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, and Domain-Driven Design for maintainable systems.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 32 potential issues including external_commands, env_access, and weak cryptographic algorithms. After evaluation, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill contains only documentation and Python code examples for software architecture patterns. No actual command execution, cryptographic code, or secret access exists. The flagged patterns are innocent uses of architecture terminology (domain, ports, adapters) and placeholder interface definitions.

high_findings.title: False Positive: env_access Pattern
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'api_key' parameter in StripePaymentAdapter as env_access. This is a legitimate interface definition, not actual secret access. The code only defines type signatures for a payment adapter.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged code is a Python type annotation 'def __init__(self, api_key: str):' - a placeholder for how to initialize a Stripe client, not actual secret exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: external_commands Pattern
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but the files contain only Python code examples and markdown documentation. No backtick shell execution exists.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains directory structure examples using backticks for markdown formatting, not shell command execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'domain' and 'bounded context' as system reconnaissance. These are legitimate Domain-Driven Design (DDD) terminology, not system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: In DDD, 'domain' refers to business domain, not network domain. 'Bounded context' is a DDD pattern for delineating business boundaries.

content.value_statement: Build maintainable, testable backend systems using proven architecture patterns. This skill helps you apply Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, and Domain-Driven Design to create scalable software.
actual_capabilities: Guide selection of appropriate architecture pattern based on project requirements
Implement Clean Architecture with proper layer separation (entities, use cases, adapters)
Set up Hexagonal Architecture with ports and adapters pattern
Apply Domain-Driven Design tactical patterns (entities, value objects, aggregates)
Define bounded contexts and context mappings for complex domains
Create testable code structures independent of frameworks
limitations: Does not generate full application code from scratch
Does not provide implementation for specific frameworks or databases
Does not include deployment or infrastructure configuration
Does not provide runtime validation or testing

use_cases.title: Architect New Backend System
use_cases.description: Design a new backend service using Clean Architecture with proper layer separation and dependency rules.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers and architects designing new systems

use_cases.title: Refactor Monolithic Codebase
use_cases.description: Migrate tightly coupled code to hexagonal architecture with clear ports and adapters.

use_cases.title: Apply DDD Tactical Patterns
use_cases.description: Implement domain models using entities, value objects, and aggregates for complex business domains.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Architecture Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a Clean Architecture structure for an e-commerce application. What layers should I create and what are the dependencies between them?
prompt_templates.scenario: When starting a new backend project and needing architecture guidance

prompt_templates.title: Hexagonal Architecture Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to implement a payment service using Hexagonal Architecture. Include ports and adapters with a Stripe example.
prompt_templates.scenario: When implementing a service that needs external integrations

prompt_templates.title: Domain-Driven Design Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DDD domain model for an order management system. Include entities, value objects, aggregates, and domain events.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Migration Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a Rails monolith. How do I migrate it step by step to Clean Architecture without doing a full rewrite?
prompt_templates.scenario: When planning incremental migration of legacy systems

output_examples.input: Design a Clean Architecture structure for a user management system
output: Layer 1: Domain - User entity with business rules (activation, validation)
Layer 2: Use Cases - CreateUser, UpdateUser, DeactivateUser

output_examples.input: Explain the difference between entities and value objects
output: Entities: Have identity, mutable, equality by ID (e.g., Order, User)
Value Objects: No identity, immutable, equality by attributes (e.g., Email, Money, Address)

best_practices: Keep business logic in the domain layer, independent of frameworks
Define clear interfaces (ports) before implementing adapters
Ensure inner layers know nothing about outer layers
anti_patterns: Putting business logic in controllers (fat controllers)
Mixing domain logic with infrastructure code
Creating anemic domain models with no behavior

faq.question: When should I use Clean Architecture?
faq.answer: Use Clean Architecture for complex backend systems where testability and maintainability are priorities. It adds initial overhead so avoid for simple CRUD applications.

faq.question: What is the main benefit of Hexagonal Architecture?
faq.answer: It allows you to swap implementations easily. You can use a mock payment adapter for testing and switch to Stripe for production without changing domain logic.

faq.question: How is Domain-Driven Design different from Clean Architecture?
faq.answer: DDD focuses on modeling complex business domains with bounded contexts and ubiquitous language. Clean Architecture is a technical structure that can work with or without DDD.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes, the patterns are language-agnostic. The examples use Python but the principles apply to any object-oriented language.

faq.question: What are value objects and when should I use them?
faq.answer: Value objects are immutable objects defined by their attributes, not identity. Use them for concepts like money, email, or address where equality matters more than identity.

faq.question: How do I start migrating a legacy codebase?
faq.answer: Start by identifying domain boundaries. Create new code in the target architecture while keeping legacy code around. Migrate incrementally feature by feature.
